basics.name: Teo Schnell
basics.label: Aerospace Engineering Student
basics.summary: Aerospace engineering student with experience in UAV design, systems integration, manufacturing, material testing, and STEM tutoring.

work.name: Cabrillo College Robotics
work.summary: Led UAV airframe design, simulation, and system integration for the college robotics team.
highlights: Led development of a UAV platform including airframe design, simulation, and system integration
Designed and tested UAV airframes using SolidWorks Flow Simulation and flight testing
Simulated UAV designs and sensors using Gazebo Simulator and ROS2

work.name: 3D-Printed UAV Project
work.summary: Developed a custom flight stabilization system for a 3D-printed UAV.
highlights: Developed and tested a custom STM32-based flight controller with integrated sensors
Implemented PID-driven stabilization algorithms for flight control
Built and validated a 3D-printed UAV airframe

work.name: Thermodynamics Gas Simulation
work.summary: Created a particle-based gas simulation to model temperature effects.
highlights: Created a particle-based simulation modeling gas behavior and temperature effects

work.name: RC Plane Physics Model
work.summary: Built a physics model to predict RC aircraft performance with high accuracy.
highlights: Built a Python model predicting thrust, drag, and lift for RC aircraft
Achieved model accuracy within 3% error compared to real-world data

work.name: University of California Santa Cruz
work.summary: Selected for an NSF-funded Research Experience for Undergraduates program.
highlights: Selected for the UC Santa Cruz Materials Science REU program for summer 2026

work.name: Cabrillo College Physics Learning Center
work.summary: Tutored students in physics and engineering concepts.
highlights: Assisted students in mastering complex physics and engineering concepts
Tutored mechanics, circuits, and thermodynamics coursework

work.name: Cabrillo College
work.summary: Tutored students in computer science and STEM coursework.
highlights: Tutored students in C++, Python, MATLAB, RISC-V, and mathematics
Became the youngest CS tutor in Cabrillo College history at age 16

work.name: Cabrillo Robotics Foundation
work.summary: Co-founded and led a nonprofit robotics foundation.
highlights: Co-founded and served as president of Cabrillo Robotics Foundation
Led strategy, outreach, and nonprofit operations

work.name: Cabrillo Robotics Club
work.summary: Served as president of the Cabrillo Robotics Club.
highlights: Led club meetings, projects, and student engagement
